---
title: Status of Fine Particluate Matter in Seoul.
description: 서울의 미세먼지
author: ''
date: '2021-01-13'
slug: welcome
categories: []
coverImage: //res.cloudinary.com/ynot-gallery/image/upload/v1610006599/samples/landscapes/girl-urban-view.jpg
coverMeta: out
#coverImage: //d1u9biwaxjngwg.cloudfront.net/cover-image-showcase/city.jpg
#coverMeta: out
# Featured image
# To use, add an image named `featured.jpg/png` to your page's folder.
# Focal points: Smart, Center, TopLeft, Top, TopRight, Left, Right, BottomLeft, Bottom, BottomRight.
tags:
- cover image
thumbnailImage: //res.cloudinary.com/ynot-gallery/image/upload/v1610006701/featured_pl5sn3.png
thumbnailImagePosition: left
metaAlignment: left
---
>*영어 문장 연습을 위한 보고서 COPY로 문제시 삭제

## Status of Fine Particulate Matter in Seoul (2004-2020)


|  Research Institute of Public Health and Environment of Seoul Metropolitan Goverment operates a network of air monitoring stations that measure fine particulate matter and other air pollutants. This indicator reports on the concentration of fine particulate matter from 2004-2020 and compares it to the South Korea Air Quality Standards established by Ministers of the Environment.
####
* **Fine particulate matter**: Solid or liquid particles floating in the air are called particluate matter. The smallest of these particles(those that are 2.5 microns or less in diameter, or less than 1/20th the widh of a human hair) are called fine particluate matter(PM2.5). Fine particluate matter comes from many natural and huamn activites, includeing wildfires and emission from prescribed burning, forestry operations, residential woodstoves, and transportation.

* **Be Harmful to humans**: Exposure to fine particlulate matter has been associated with several serious health effects including heart and lug disease. Both shor-term(24-hour) and longer-term(a year or more) exposure to fine particluate matter can have negative effects on human health.

The map below summarises South Korea Ambient Air Quality Standard achievement status for fine particluate matter in Seoul. air zones, as well as both the annual and 24-hour PM2.5 levels at individual monitoring stations. Summaries are given for each monitoring station where sufficient data was available for the 200402720 reporting period.

### **Fine Particluate Matter(PM2.5)**: South Korea Ambient Air Quality Stnadard

|National ambient air|quality Standard|
|:---:|:---|
|Yearly|15ug/m3 or less|
|24-hour|35ug/m3 or less|
